topic: vLLM vs Ollama for Production LLM Serving

competitors.title: vLLM vs Ollama: Which is Better for LLM Deployment?
key_points: vLLM optimized for high-throughput production serving with PagedAttention
Ollama optimized for local development and ease of use
vLLM supports OpenAI-compatible API server out of the box
Ollama excels at quick local model management and switching
Different use cases: vLLM = production API, Ollama = local dev/personal use
competitors.unique_angle: General comparison but lacks production benchmarks and cost analysis

competitors.title: Serving LLMs at Scale: vLLM Production Best Practices
key_points: vLLM OpenAI-compatible server with PagedAttention for efficient memory management
Supports tensor parallelism, pipeline parallelism for multi-GPU serving
Continuous batching and prefix caching for throughput optimization
Quantization support: AWQ, GPTQ, SqueezeLLM for reduced VRAM usage
Production features: health checks, metrics, logging, graceful shutdown
competitors.unique_angle: Official docs — reference-style, not a comparison or tutorial

competitors.title: Ollama: Run LLMs Locally Without the Hassle
key_points: Single command model download and execution: ollama run llama3
Model library with 100+ popular models pre-configured
Modelfile system for custom model configuration
REST API compatibility for integration with apps
Multi-modal support (LLaVA, Llava-Next, etc.)
competitors.unique_angle: Official blog — focuses on simplicity and developer experience, not production scaling

competitors.title: vLLM vs Text Generation Inference vs Ollama: A Comprehensive Comparison
key_points: vLLM achieves highest throughput for concurrent requests with continuous batching
Ollama has lowest barrier to entry but lacks production features
TGI (HuggingFace) provides middle ground with good OpenAI API compatibility
Benchmark results: vLLM 2-4x higher throughput than Ollama for concurrent users
Memory efficiency comparison across GPU configurations
competitors.unique_angle: Three-way comparison with actual benchmarks, but benchmarks may be dated (pre-2026)

competitors.title: Running LLMs in Production: vLLM Deployment Guide 2026
key_points: Photoroom's real-world vLLM deployment serving millions of requests daily
Kubernetes-based auto-scaling with GPU node pools
Prefix caching reduced latency by 40% for their use case
Monitoring with Prometheus + Grafana dashboards
Cost optimization: spot GPU instances with graceful degradation
competitors.unique_angle: Real production case study from Photoroom with actual scaling and cost numbers

stats.stat: vLLM GitHub: 77,501 stars, 15,892 forks as of April 2026
Ollama GitHub: 169,569 stars, 15,712 forks as of April 2026
vLLM achieves 2-4x higher throughput than Ollama for concurrent request loads with continuous batching
vLLM PagedAttention reduces GPU memory waste from 60-80% to <4% compared to naive batching
vLLM supports tensor parallelism up to 8+ GPUs for serving 70B+ parameter models
Ollama supports 100+ models in its model library with one-command installation
vLLM prefix caching can reduce latency by up to 40% for repeated system prompts

angles.angle: Production decision framework: When to use vLLM vs Ollama vs both
angles.reason: Most comparisons treat it as either/or. In reality, many teams use Ollama for dev and vLLM for production. A decision matrix for different stages would fill a real gap.

angles.angle: Cost of ownership: Running vLLM vs Ollama in production for a year
angles.reason: No one has calculated the actual TCO (GPU costs, engineering time, monitoring, scaling) for each tool. This would be highly valuable for engineering leads making infrastructure decisions.

angles.angle: From Ollama to vLLM: Migration guide when you outgrow local development
angles.reason: Many teams start with Ollama and need to scale. No guide exists for this transition — model compatibility, API migration, configuration mapping.

angles.angle: vLLM Kubernetes deployment: Production auto-scaling with GPU node pools
angles.reason: Photoroom is the only production case study. A generalizable Kubernetes deployment guide would capture the enterprise audience.

angles.angle: Benchmarking vLLM vs Ollama in 2026: Throughput, latency, and memory across GPU tiers
angles.reason: Existing benchmarks are from 2024-early 2025. Both tools have had major updates. Fresh benchmarks on current hardware (H100, A100, L40S) would be highly referenced.
